deck_goal: Present a data-driven go-to-market plan for launching AnalyticsPro in the North American mid-market segment.
audience: VP of Product and Growth leadership deciding Q3 launch sequencing

sections.heading: Market Size Timeline
paragraphs: The North American mid-market analytics segment grew from $2.1B in 2022 to $3.4B in 2024, with projected CAGR of 28% through 2027. Self-serve analytics adoption doubled year-over-year in the 100-500 employee segment.
claims: The mid-market analytics TAM reached $3.4B in 2024 with 28% projected CAGR through 2027.

sections.heading: Competitive Landscape
paragraphs: Incumbent tools like Tableau and Looker dominate enterprise but leave mid-market underserved on pricing and onboarding speed. New entrants compete on time-to-value: average onboarding dropped from 6 weeks to 3 days for modern platforms.
claims: Mid-market buyers prioritize time-to-value over feature depth, creating an opening for platforms with sub-week onboarding.

sections.heading: Launch Sequence
paragraphs: Phase 1: invite-only beta with 50 design partners (July). Phase 2: public launch with self-serve signup and PLG motion (September). Phase 3: channel partner enablement and outbound sales overlay (November).
claims: A three-phase launch sequence from beta to PLG to channel partners de-risks market entry while building social proof.

sections.heading: Pricing and Margins
paragraphs: Target ACV of $18K positions AnalyticsPro below enterprise incumbents ($50K+) but above freemium tools ($0-5K). Gross margin at 82% with CAC payback under 12 months at projected conversion rates of 8% from free trial.
claims: An $18K ACV with 82% gross margin and sub-12-month CAC payback creates a sustainable mid-market unit economics profile.

sections.heading: Beta NPS and Feature Requests
paragraphs: Beta cohort NPS averaged 62 across 38 respondents. Top feature requests: Slack integration (71%), scheduled reports (64%), and custom dashboards (58%). Median time-to-first-insight was 47 minutes, beating the 2-hour target by 60%.
claims: Beta NPS of 62 and 47-minute time-to-first-insight validate the product-market fit hypothesis for mid-market self-serve analytics.
